pattern: A value can never qualify if any single card shows it on both faces; among all remaining values the smallest is the answer, found by collecting the forbidden set first.
hints: Think about which numbers can never be made to appear only on a hidden face.
If a card has the same number on its front and its back, that number is always visible no matter how you flip, so it is disqualified.
Collect every value that appears on both faces of the same card into a forbidden set; then scan all values across both arrays and return the smallest one not in that set, or 0 if none remains.

edge_inputs.description: every value is matched on a card so no good integer exists
single card with differing faces gives the smaller value
smallest value is forbidden so a larger value wins
value forbidden on one card but the same value free on backs is still forbidden
all faces distinct so the global minimum is the answer
front and back identical everywhere returns zero

stress.description: random cards over a small value range to force many forbidden and candidate clashes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    fronts = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    backs = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    return [fronts, backs]
stress.note: narrow range makes forbidden values common
